content.full_text:
September 14, 1976

Ms. Susan Walenta
El Cerrito Democratic Club
Post Office Box 192
El Cerrito, California 94530

Dear Ms. Walenta:

Thank you for your letter of September 2, 1976 concerning the
FPPC's regulations concerning reporting requirements of polit-
ical clubs under the Political Reform Act.

Enclosed are FPPC regulations 18215 and 18225 outlining contri-
butions and expenditures together with examples which explain
how the regulations apply to political clubs.

We will be holding an education seminar in Oakland on September
20, 1976 at 11:00 a.m. where this information will be reviewed
in detail. I have enclosed a letter with a coupon you should
complete and return if you plan to attend.

I hope this information will be helpful, if not, please do not
hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistant Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

P O BOX 192 EL CERRITO CA 94530

■ EL CERRITO CCDC DEMOCRATIC CLUB ■ FOUNDED 1952 ■

September 2, 1976


Ms. Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Siciliano-Kutchins:

     Reference is made to my letter of January 26, 1976, and
yours of February 18, 1976, wherein you stated that you would
answer my questions concerning the status of political clubs
under Proposition 9 after the Commission had decided the matter.

     I have never received any other response or answers to my
questions from your office. However, I was told by some people
in the California Democratic Council that they thought that
political clubs were required to report if their income from
fund raising events exceeded $500, irrespective of any contri-
butions they might make, but that income from dues was not
counted in reaching the $500 limit. Is this correct? If so,
is that gross or net income?

     Our club plans to hold its annual dinner next month. There-
fore, I need to have answers to my questions as soon as possible.

     Thank you very much for your assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Susan Walenta
                                        President

[illegible] 7 02

/
